<!-- generated by scripts/sync_agents.mjs -->
# Command index

This file is generated from `skills/*/SKILL.md`. Edit the skill, then run `npm run sync`.

## `/office-hours`
The user is overwhelmed, directionless, juggling multiple student problems, or asking a broad question like "how do I get the most out of college?"

## `/academic-record-repair`
The user needs to fix a transcript error, grade dispute, missing credit, prerequisite mistake, attendance record issue, or other academic record problem.

## `/anonymous-advice`
The user wants to run an anonymous advice column, confessions page, question box, or submission-based campus media project.

## `/build-project`
The user wants to build something but the idea is still foggy, too broad, or mostly identity talk.

## `/bureaucracy-game`
The user is blocked by forms, deadlines, fines, petitions, housing/admin offices, aid offices, registrar-style gatekeepers, or any other institutional sludge.

## `/campus-map`
The user is new, bored, isolated, or wants to understand where the useful people, rooms, rituals, and hidden resources actually are.

## `/campus-newspaper`
The user wants to start or relaunch a campus paper, newsletter, media account, reporting project, or student publication with an actual beat.

## `/cold-outreach`
The user wants a reply from a stranger or weak tie and needs help choosing the target, the ask, and the proof.

## `/course-registration-and-drop`
The user needs a tactical class schedule, registration strategy, add/drop plan, override ask, or withdrawal decision.

## `/create-presentation`
The user needs to build a class deck, talk, pitch, or presentation that people can actually follow and remember.

## `/dating`
The user wants help asking someone out, reading a dating situation, getting unstuck around attraction, or becoming less passive and weird about it.

## `/design-your-own-major`
The user wants to create a custom major, concentration, self-designed path, or approval packet because the default majors are too small or too fake.

## `/foia-campus`
The user wants records, emails, contracts, salary data, logs, meeting materials, or other documents that explain how an institution actually works.

## `/follow-curiosity`
The user feels restless, bored, obsessed, or vaguely alive around something and needs to turn that signal into action.

## `/free-food`
The user wants to find free food, lower food costs, build a meal radar, or turn food intelligence into a useful public resource.

## `/fuckclubs`
The user is deciding whether to join, leave, ignore, or replace a club or campus organization.

## `/fuckgeneds`
The user wants an ethical survival plan for a boring, badly designed, or low-value class.

## `/fund-student-project`
The user needs money, materials, travel support, hosting, printing, food, or sponsorship for a student project or event.

## `/get-people-to-care`
The user built something, is launching something, or is tired of nobody showing up or paying attention.

## `/housing-contract-rework`
The user is stuck in a dorm, apartment, or housing contract and wants to cancel, transfer, sublet, replace, or reduce the damage.

## `/internship-hunt`
The user wants internships, jobs, summer direction, portfolio strategy, or a plan to get near real work.

## `/intl-student-systems`
The user is an international student dealing with visa rules, work limits, campus paperwork, housing, travel, social adaptation, summer plans, or opportunity strategy.

## `/jailbreak-summer`
The user is planning their summer and wants something more alive than default internship drift.

## `/jailbroken`
The user is asking a broad meta-question about college, direction, meaning, or "what should I do," and the real issue is likely fog, fear, overcompliance, borrowed desire, or a dead strategic frame.

## `/launch-campus-tool`
The user wants to build a campus app, site, bot, map, archive, directory, data tool, or workflow tool.

## `/make-friends`
The user is lonely, socially stuck, new in town, a commuter, a transfer, off-campus, international, or tired of weak ties that never become real.

## `/make-me-alive`
The user feels flat, bored, isolated, over-optimized, or unable to tell what they want anymore.

## `/meal-swipe-market`
The user wants to build a meal-swipe donation, exchange, matching, or visibility system.

## `/meet-interesting-people`
The user wants better people around them, more alive conversations, collaborators or mentors, or escape from dead social loops.

## `/money-mode`
The user is under money pressure, needs fast relief, wants to find hidden aid or waivers, or needs a student money system that actually reduces stress.

## `/opportunity-audit`
The user is deciding whether to pursue, accept, reject, compare, or extract value from an internship, job, club, lab, fellowship, campus program, or other "good on paper" opportunity.

## `/parking-and-ticket-resolution`
The user got a parking ticket, permit issue, tow notice, transit fine, or campus parking dispute and wants the cleanest way to fight it.

## `/pick-professor`
The user wants to choose professors, classes, mentors, or office-hours targets based on people rather than labels.

## `/publish-something-real`
The user wants to start a publication, account, newsletter, blog, guide, interview series, or media project.

## `/reading-list`
The user wants to know what to read, build a reading life, create a reading stack for a project or life direction, or stop reading random sludge online.

## `/research-radar`
The user wants to find research directions, labs, professors, or serious reading paths worth chasing.

## `/salary-guide`
The user wants to expose or explain who gets paid what at the university, or build a searchable salary transparency project.

## `/status-detox`
The user feels pulled by prestige, comparison, club hierarchy, internships, labs, elite scenes, or "good on paper" decisions and cannot tell what they actually want.

## `/student-projects-archive`
The user wants a public archive of student projects, campus creations, experiments, clubs-that-did-real-things, or project memory.

## `/sublease-exchange`
The user wants to build a sublease marketplace, housing board, roommate finder, or short-term campus housing system.

## `/syllabus-archive`
The user wants a public syllabus archive, course comparison tool, professor-intel index, or class-expectations database.

## `/throw-cool-event`
The user wants to host a campus event, scene, dinner, talk, ritual, pop-up, screening, run, or conference.

## `/what-to-study`
The user is choosing classes, majors, minors, concentrations, transcript strategy, or academic direction.

## `/write-professor-email`
The user needs a short, high-stakes email to a professor, TA, admin, founder, alum, organizer, or other adult.

Aliases: `/write-sharp-email`
